creator: _jkrow
caption:
If you’re wondering what souvenirs to buy in Brussels, Belgium, I gotchu (not shown: chocolate)

I usually don’t spend a lot of money when I’m home, but my budget goes straight out the window as soon as I take a trip, because the high of finding beautiful handicrafts abroad is REAL 😅 I also love supporting small businesses and local artisans. 

Souvenir tour soon?

transcription.text: spent all day drinking Aperol and shopping. I have nobody to show my finds to you, so I really just want to show you what I got. Okay, first thing I got in Brussels was some lace. When we travel, I always like to find the most like authentic handicraft or souvenir that region is known for. Belgium is actually famous for lace. I found this one shop that has been selling lace for decades and decades. It is handmade, done with like a bobbin. I'm going to frame it and put it with like the rest of my souvenirs. I'll have to do like a little souvenir tour at some point in time. Let me show you the next thing. Next thing is this wool and leather bag. It was made in Belgium by a Belgian designer. I'm just literally obsessed with it. I paid way too much money for this. I love it. So, what do you think? Okay, and finally, I found this print by like a printing company in France. Oh, I love this print. Got it for our bathroom. It's currently being renovated, and I think it's just going to go great on the wall, and then I can be like, oh yeah, I got that in Belgium, and it's from France. So yeah, I love decorating our house with things that we get abroad. And that's all. Hooray!
